import { PoseLandmarker, FilesetResolver } from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@mediapipe/tasks-vision@0.10.14/vision_bundle.mjs";

/* ---------- CONFIG / PLACEHOLDER ASSETS ---------- */
// No real ballet footage was supplied, so a public-domain placeholder clip stands

// clips (each representing one figure) when they are available.
const PLACEHOLDER_CLIPS = [
  "https://interactive-examples.mdn.mozilla.net/media/cc0-videos/flower.mp4"
];

/* ---------- STATE ---------- */
let rulesChecked = new Set();

/* ---------- LEFT TAB: RULES ---------- */
window.toggleRule = function(el){
  const key = el.dataset.rule;
  const cb = el.querySelector("input");
  if(rulesChecked.has(key)){
    rulesChecked.delete(key);
    cb.checked = false;
    el.classList.remove("checked");
  } else {
    rulesChecked.add(key);
    cb.checked = true;
    el.classList.add("checked");
  }
  document.getElementById("ruleProgress").textContent =
    `${rulesChecked.size} / 3 rules confirmed` +
    (rulesChecked.size < 3 ? " — complete all to unlock the Reference Player." : " — Reference Player unlocked!");
  if(rulesChecked.size === 3){
    document.getElementById("leftTab2Btn").classList.remove("locked");
  } else {
    document.getElementById("leftTab2Btn").classList.add("locked");
    if(document.getElementById("leftTab2").classList.contains("active")) switchLeftTab(1);
  }
};

window.switchLeftTab = function(n){
  if(n === 2 && rulesChecked.size < 5) return;
  document.getElementById("leftTab1").classList.toggle("active", n===1);
  document.getElementById("leftTab2").classList.toggle("active", n===2);
  document.getElementById("leftTab1Btn").classList.toggle("active", n===1);
  document.getElementById("leftTab2Btn").classList.toggle("active", n===2);
};

/* ---------- LEFT TAB: REFERENCE PLAYER ---------- */
const refVideo = document.getElementById("refVideo");
const refScrub = document.getElementById("refScrub");

refVideo.addEventListener("timeupdate", () => {
  if(refVideo.duration) refScrub.value = (refVideo.currentTime / refVideo.duration) * 100;
});
refScrub.addEventListener("input", () => {
  if(refVideo.duration) refVideo.currentTime = (refScrub.value/100) * refVideo.duration;
});

window.toggleRefPlay = function(){
  if(refVideo.paused){ refVideo.play(); document.getElementById("refPlayBtn").textContent = "⏸ Pause"; }
  else { refVideo.pause(); document.getElementById("refPlayBtn").textContent = "▶ Play"; }
};
window.resetRef = function(){
  refVideo.pause();
  refVideo.currentTime = 0;
  document.getElementById("refPlayBtn").textContent = "▶ Play";
};

window.generateSequence = function(){
  // Stitches PLACEHOLDER_CLIPS end-to-end into the reference player (simple
  // sequential-src approach — a real backend would concat/transcode into one file).
  let idx = 0;
  refVideo.src = PLACEHOLDER_CLIPS[idx];
  refVideo.onended = () => {
    idx++;
    if(idx < PLACEHOLDER_CLIPS.length){ refVideo.src = PLACEHOLDER_CLIPS[idx]; refVideo.play(); }
  };
  document.getElementById("refStatus").textContent =
    `Sequence generated (${PLACEHOLDER_CLIPS.length} clip stitched). Projected into Left Panel player.`;
  document.getElementById("camStatus").textContent = "Sequence ready. Click the ● trigger to start standby.";
};

const camCanvas = document.getElementById("camCanvas");

const camFrame = document.getElementById("camFrame");
const countdownOverlay = document.getElementById("countdownOverlay");

content.text:
All four requests are in:

1. A "Sequence Builder" panel now sits above Generate Sequence with an exercise-count input.
2. A Turnout/Technique focus toggle picks the clip pool — Turnout draws from tendu, coupé, passé (up to 9 exercises); Technique draws from plié, rond de jambe, rond de jambe en dedans (up to 5). The count input's max auto-adjusts to whichever pool is selected.
3. The Reference Player now preloads every clip's duration before playing and tracks a single continuous timeline across the whole stitched sequence — the scrubber and a new time readout (e.g. "0:14 / 1:32") reflect total elapsed/total duration, and dragging the scrubber correctly jumps to the right clip and offset rather than resetting per movement.
4. The plié clip switches to `object-fit: contain` while it's playing, so it shows the full frame instead of the tight crop `cover` was giving it — reverts to normal fill for every other clip.

I also caught and fixed a real compatibility bug along the way: 7 of your 14 uploaded clips (all the "right"-side ones) were encoded in HEVC/H.265, which most browsers other than Safari can't play — I transcoded them to H.264 so the sequence won't silently break on Chrome/Firefox.

All five changes are in:

1. Plié now uses `object-fit: contain` plus a `scale(1.55)` zoom — a middle ground between the original crop (too tight) and plain contain (too far out).
2. The calendar's old auto-schedule scheme is gone. There's now a Daily Checklist panel below the calendar listing all 14 exercises; click any day to view its checklist, and each day's cell shows an x/14 completion count.
3. Checklist items are only ticked when a camera-scored take of the Reference Player sequence hits 75%+ — the exercises attempted in that take get checked off for today; a sub-75% take checks off nothing.
4. A streak banner sits above the calendar. The moment a day's checklist reaches 14/14 it registers a streak completion — continuing the streak if it's within 30 hours of the last one, resetting to 1 if more than 30 hours have passed. If 30+ hours pass with no new full day, the streak displays as 0.
5. From hour 25 to hour 30 since the last full completion, the banner shows an ⏳ with a "resets soon" message, and the same hourglass appears on today's calendar cell — both refresh live every minute.
